summary: The test suite is well-rounded and covers all key requirements: version parsing (txt and package.json), bump-type detection for all three commit categories (feat/fix/breaking), version stepping with reset semantics, changelog generation with section grouping, file updates, and an integration layer that exercises all three bump paths plus the no-op case. Workflow structure validation (file existence, trigger events, script path, actionlint) is a nice inclusion. Edge cases are handled meaningfully: empty commit list, leading 'v' prefix, missing file, invalid semver content, BREAKING CHANGE in body vs '!' marker, package.json key preservation. Gaps that prevent a 5: the commit-log delimiter (---) is implicit in fixtures but never unit-tested as a parsing contract; the Invoke-VersionBumper.ps1 CLI wrapper has no dedicated tests; malformed package.json, empty version file, and changelog-prepend-vs-overwrite behaviour are untested. Design is clean — GUID-based temp file names prevent collision, BeforeEach/AfterEach properly isolate integration cases, and the actionlint test gracefully skips when the tool is absent — but the repeated 'create temp file / finally remove' pattern across five unit tests could be factored into a shared helper to reduce noise.
